finding: Homepage proof stats all render as zero: "0+" entrepreneurs launched, "$0M+" sales, "0+" vending locations

evidence.quote:
0+
ENTREPRENEURS LAUNCHED
$0M+
SNACK/DRINK SALES IN VENDINGPRENEUR MACHINES
0+
VENDING LOCATIONS W/OUR GUIDANCE

persona_rationale: I'm deciding in seconds whether these people are legit. Stats showing 0 read as either broken or as 'nobody has actually done this' — it kills the credibility right where the page is trying to build it, and I won't risk my limited time on something that looks unproven.

suggested_fix.proposed: Populate real figures (e.g. "500+ entrepreneurs launched", "$12M+ sales") or remove the stat band entirely until real numbers exist

finding: Homepage is a very long single scroll of ~15 testimonials before the final 'Take Action' CTA
persona_rationale: With two free minutes I can't wade through fifteen long testimonials to find the next step. The page should let me reach the decision point fast, not bury it under social proof I don't have time to read.

suggested_fix.current: All ~15 full-length testimonials stacked inline before the closing CTA
suggested_fix.proposed: Show 3-4 testimonials with a 'See more stories' toggle, and place a CTA higher up so the action is reachable without a long scroll

finding: Homepage layout shift (CLS 0.263) causes content to jump while loading
persona_rationale: When the page jumps as I'm reaching for a button, I lose my place and have to re-orient — small, but it adds to the feeling that the site is fighting me when I'm already rushed.

suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve space for the hero image and partner logos (explicit width/height) to bring CLS under 0.1

finding: Mobile navigation is an icon-only hamburger with no text label
persona_rationale: On my phone the hamburger is the only door to every page; it's fine, but an unlabeled icon is one more thing I have to recognise rather than read when my attention is split.

suggested_fix.current: Hamburger icon with no accessible/visible label
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Menu' label or aria-label to the hamburger button

finding: Three required fields are commitments I may not have made yet: Business stage, Available startup budget, and Launch timeline are all required
persona_rationale: I'm exploring vending, not committing. Forcing me to pick a budget band and a launch month before I can even ask a question makes me feel pre-qualified out, and an overwhelmed person reads that as 'not for me yet' and closes the tab.

suggested_fix.current: Business stage, budget, and timeline marked '(required)'
suggested_fix.proposed: Make only Name + Email required; mark stage/budget/timeline optional with a 'Not sure yet' option in each dropdown

finding: After submitting, the only confirmation is a faint green inline line beside the button; the form does not clear or redirect, so the success state is easy to miss

evidence.quote: Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.
the automation itself missed the success state and re-submitted 5 more times

persona_rationale: If I look up to serve a customer and look back, I can't tell whether it sent — the message is small, off to the side, and the filled-in form still sits there. I'd assume it failed and either re-submit or give up, exactly like the automation did.

suggested_fix.current: Small green inline text beside the button; form stays filled, no redirect
suggested_fix.proposed: Redirect to the existing /thank-you-for-applying page, or show a full-width success panel that replaces the form

finding: A dedicated /thank-you-for-applying confirmation page exists but the apply form never routes to it

evidence.quote: A dedicated `/thank-you-for-applying` page EXISTS in the app but the apply form does not route to it.

persona_rationale: The clear, reassuring confirmation I needed already exists in the app — it just isn't connected to the form. Sending me there would instantly fix my biggest doubt about whether the application went through.

suggested_fix.current: Apply form shows inline text and stays on /apply

finding: No progress saving on the apply form — an interruption that reloads the tab loses all entered data
persona_rationale: My day is constant interruptions. If a customer pulls me away and the tab reloads, retyping name, city, and four dropdowns is enough friction that I just won't come back to it.

suggested_fix.current: Uncontrolled single-shot form with no persistence
suggested_fix.proposed: Persist field values to localStorage as the user types and restore them on reload

finding: Validation only fires on submit, one native tooltip at a time ('Please fill out this field.'), so required fields aren't signposted upfront
persona_rationale: I want to know before I start which fields will block me, not discover them one tooltip at a time after I think I'm done. Drip-fed errors waste the minutes I don't have.

suggested_fix.current: Native per-field tooltip shown only on submit
suggested_fix.proposed: Show inline 'required' indicators and validate on blur so I see all gaps at once

finding: On mobile the submit button is far below the fold after a tall hero, with no sticky CTA
persona_rationale: On my phone I scroll past a huge headline before the form even starts, then keep scrolling to find Submit. A rushed user loses confidence about how much is left to do.

suggested_fix.current: Tall hero pushes form down; submit button only reachable after long scroll
suggested_fix.proposed: Tighten the mobile hero and add a sticky 'Submit application' bar once the form is in view

finding: There is no 'Contact' link in the header navigation — it appears only in the footer

evidence.quote:
navigation "Primary":
    - link "About"
    - link "Resources"
    - link "Case Studies"
    - link "News"
link is in the FOOTER only — "CONTACT US"; not in the header nav

persona_rationale: When I have a quick question I look to the top of the page for 'Contact'. Not finding it there means scrolling all the way to the bottom to hunt for it — friction at the exact moment I want help fast.

suggested_fix.current: Contact only linked from the footer
suggested_fix.proposed: Add 'Contact' to the primary header navigation

finding: Required Message field gives no guidance on what to include or expected length
persona_rationale: Faced with an empty required box when I'm rushed, I freeze on what to write. A short prompt would let me fire off a question in seconds.

suggested_fix.current: Empty Message textarea with no placeholder
suggested_fix.proposed: Add placeholder guidance, e.g. 'Tell us where you are and your main question (a sentence or two is fine)'

finding: The section is labelled 'NEWS' in the nav and URL but the page heading is 'BLOG'

evidence.quote: Page is headed "BLOG" while the nav item that leads here says "NEWS"

persona_rationale: Two names for one place makes me pause and wonder if I clicked the wrong thing — a tiny doubt, but my tired brain doesn't have spare capacity to reconcile it.

suggested_fix.current: Nav 'NEWS' leads to a page headed 'BLOG'
suggested_fix.proposed: Use one name everywhere — make the page heading match the nav label ('News')

finding: Above the fold the article cards render as empty outlines, one showing raw alt text instead of an image, so the page looks broken/unfinished on first paint

evidence.quote: Top 10 Profitable Products to Stock in Your Vending Machine (Snacks, Healthy Items, Niche Products)
they render as BLANK card outlines until scrolled into view

persona_rationale: My first glance tells me whether to trust a page. Empty cards and visible alt text read as 'this didn't load' — I'd assume the site is broken and leave rather than wait or scroll.

suggested_fix.current: Scroll-reveal cards paint blank; a 500'd image shows alt text as body copy
suggested_fix.proposed: Render card content immediately (no blank scroll-reveal state) and add image fallbacks so a failed thumbnail shows a placeholder, not raw alt text

finding: A thumbnail image returns HTTP 500, leaving a card with no picture
persona_rationale: A missing/broken image on the very first content I see signals neglect — if they can't keep a picture loading, can I trust them with my application?

suggested_fix.current: Article thumbnail 500s, card renders imageless with alt text
suggested_fix.proposed: Fix the image source/optimizer config and add a fallback image for failed loads

finding: Article page loads slowly (~1.7s DCL) with CLS 0.263, so content shifts while reading
persona_rationale: If the text jumps as I start reading, I lose my line and give up faster — every second of instability spends patience I don't have.

suggested_fix.current: ~2s load and CLS 0.263 on article pages
suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve image dimensions and reduce blocking work to bring CLS under 0.1 and speed first paint

finding: The thank-you page is clear and reassuring, but it is orphaned because the apply form never sends users to it

evidence.quote:
THANKS FOR APPLYING.
The team reviews application details before the next conversation.

persona_rationale: This is exactly the calm 'you're done, here's what happens next' moment I needed after applying — the fix is simply to route me here (see finding 11-business-owner-007).

suggested_fix.current: Good confirmation page exists but is unreachable from the apply flow
suggested_fix.proposed: Wire the apply form's success path to this page

finding: Header and footer navigation links are only 17-20px tall, below comfortable touch-target size
persona_rationale: On my phone, often with flour on my fingers, tiny links are hard to hit accurately — I mis-tap and have to navigate back, wasting the minutes I came in with.

suggested_fix.proposed: Increase tappable height to at least 44px per WCAG target-size guidance
